frameworks.name: logistic regression of choice history
frameworks.one_line: dissociates effects of prior stimuli vs prior choices on current decisions

frameworks.name: reduced priors account of ASD
frameworks.one_line: theory that ASD reflects diminished influence of prior information on perception

frameworks.name: serial dependence / choice history bias
frameworks.one_line: attractive pull of recent choices reframed as consistency bias elevated in ASD

frameworks.name: multisensory cue integration
frameworks.one_line: visual-vestibular heading task used as a second testbed

core_question: Does autism spectrum disorder involve reduced priors, or specifically an altered bias from prior perceptual choices?
key_assumptions: effects of prior stimuli and prior choices can be statistically separated
logistic regression captures the relevant history weights
two distinct tasks tap a shared decision-history mechanism
manipulations: task domain (visual location discrimination vs visual-vestibular heading)
trial history of prior stimuli and prior choices
key_findings: perceptual decisions were attracted toward recent choices in both tasks
attractive choice-history bias was significantly larger in ASD across both cohorts
no group difference in the influence of prior stimuli
interpretations: ASD shows an increased consistency bias for perceptual choices rather than reduced priors
evidence challenges reduced-priors theories of autistic perception
